detaileddescription.para: Types for RSC RPC protocol messages.
See Executing Remote Procedures section in Pin User Manual

simplesect.para: APIs declared in this file are under active development and are subject to change or removal without notice

briefdescription.para: Indicates an Invalid RPC Id.

detaileddescription.para: This RPC Id is used internally

briefdescription.para: The smallest RPC Id available for Pintool developers.

detaileddescription.para: RPC Ids less than RPCID_MIN are reserved for Pin

briefdescription.para: The largest valid RPC Id available for Pintool developers.

detaileddescription.para: RPC Ids greater than RPCID_MAX are reserved for Pin

briefdescription.para: Start of reserved the low range.
End of reserved the low range.
Start of reserved the high range.
End of reserved the high range.
Check if an RPC Id is valid.
Check if an RPC Id is reserved in the low range.
Check if an RPC Id is reserved in the high range.
Check if an RPC Id is reserved.
Helper macro for creating a t_rpc_message_schema.
Helper macro for creating t_rpc_arg_schema.

parameterdescription.para: The size of the argument in bytes (for RpcRecord this is the number of members which can be at most 4096)

simplesect.para: This is a helper macro and it does not verify its inputs!

briefdescription.para: Get the type of an RPC argument schema.
Get the size of an RPC argument schema.
Maximum number of RPC arguments in a message schema.
Offset of RPC arguments from start of message.
Boolean argument type.
Integer (possibly negative) argument type.
Unsigned integer argument type.
Character argument type (char, wchar_t, char16_t, char32_t)
Floating point argument type (float, double, long double)
A memory buffer argument type.
Currently not supported.

detaileddescription.para: A record argument type that may contain members of other types A record may contain upto 4096 members. Nested records are currently not supported

briefdescription.para: An array argument which may contain upto 4096 entries of the same type.
Indicates no data argument.
The same as RpcNil.

detaileddescription.para: This type of argument is can be used to describe padding for a record argument. The decoder will use it when constructing the record layout in memory

briefdescription.para: The type of RSC RPC argument.

detaileddescription.para: This is the type we wish to encode from/decode to not the type inside the RSC RPC Message. For instance RpcInt might be encoded as int4, int12, varint, int4_neg, int12_neg or varint_neg
Does not modify argument encoding behavior

Indicate that the argument has no encodable/decodable data If this value is set on encode then the argument will be encoded as RpcNil If this value is set returning from decode then the argument does not contain valid data.

briefdescription.para: Bitwise flags affecting the encoding of RPC arguments.
The type of RSC RPC argument.

detaileddescription.para: This is the type we wish to encode from/decode to not the type inside the RSC RPC Message. For instance RpcInt might be encoded as int4, int12, varint, int4_neg, int12_neg or varint_neg

briefdescription.para: Bitwise flags affecting the encoding of RPC arguments.
Type of RPC Ids.
Type of RPC argument count.
Type of RPC argument schema.
A collection of RPC_arg_schema structures together describing an RPC message.
TStructure to hold the actual data to encode or the actual data of a decoded argument.
